---
title: "Preparation"
subtitle: "What to read and install before the workshop"
---

## Pre-Workshop Reading

Introductory materials will be distributed to registered participants ahead of the workshop. These are designed to give participants from both communities enough background to engage productively from Day 1.

::: {.callout-note}
Pre-reading papers and case study background documents will be posted here and emailed to participants approximately **4 weeks before** the workshop.
:::

### Survival Analysis Background

*To be added*

### Infectious Disease Modeling Background

*To be added*

### Cross-disciplinary Perspectives

*To be added*

---

## Software

The workshop uses **R** as the primary computing environment. Please install the following before arriving.

### R and RStudio

- [R](https://cran.r-project.org/) (≥ 4.3.0 recommended)
- [RStudio Desktop](https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/) (or another IDE of your choice)

### Key R Packages

#### Survival Analysis

```r
install.packages(c(
  "survival",    # core survival analysis
  "survminer",   # visualization
  "mstate",      # multi-state models
  "flexsurv",    # flexible parametric survival models
  "frailtyEM",   # frailty models
  "timereg",     # additive hazard models
  "cmprsk"       # competing risks
))
```

#### Infectious Disease Modeling

```r
install.packages(c(
  "EpiEstim",    # reproduction number estimation
  "EpiNow2",     # nowcasting and R estimation with delays
  "deSolve",     # ODE solvers for compartmental models
  "odin",        # fast ODE model specification
  "socialmixr"   # contact matrices for structured populations
))
```

#### General

```r
install.packages(c(
  "tidyverse",
  "data.table",
  "ggplot2",
  "patchwork",
  "knitr",
  "rmarkdown"
))
```

### Verifying Your Installation

Run the following to confirm the core packages load correctly:

```r
library(survival)
library(mstate)
library(EpiEstim)
library(deSolve)
cat("All core packages loaded successfully.\n")
```

---

## Case Study Data

The two case study datasets (A and B) will be provided to participants ahead of the workshop. They will also be available for download from this site (link to follow).

::: {.callout-warning}
Do not share the case study data externally — the complete underlying dataset is revealed only at the end of the workshop as part of the learning experience.
:::

---

## Questions?

If you have questions about preparation or software, please [open an issue](https://github.com/kylieainslie/lorentz_center_workshop/issues) on the workshop GitHub repository.
